prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "俯卧 Y 字" (prone Y raise) exercise, bird's-eye view from directly above looking down at the floor. Person posture: Athletic person LYING FACE-DOWN ON A YOGA MAT ON THE FLOOR (PRONE position) — the chest, abdomen, hips, and the front of the thighs are all pressed flat into the mat. The forehead rests on the mat, head down. Legs are extended straight together. Both arms are extended ABOVE THE HEAD and angled outward at about 45 degrees from the body axis, so the body+arms form the LETTER Y as seen from above (legs together as the stem, arms as the upper branches). Thumbs pointing toward the ceiling, palms facing each other. The arms are LIFTED A FEW CENTIMETERS off the mat by squeezing the lower trapezius. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: this person is LYING DOWN ON THE FLOOR FACE-DOWN. The person is NOT STANDING. The person is NOT UPRIGHT. The body is HORIZONTAL ON THE GROUND. Show the full body lying flat from a top-down perspective.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: lower trapezius and rear deltoid. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: rhomboids and erector spinae. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "下斜方肌" pointing to the lower trapezius; "三角肌后束" pointing to the rear deltoid; "菱形肌 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the rhomboids and erector spinae. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
